Question: Stem: (Part of above) Why will Bangladesh benefit? (i. Local interest, ii. Brand image, iii. Less corruption)
Explanation: PPP projects boost local industry and national prestige; corruption is an external factor not inherently solved by the structure.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Internal obstacles to cooperative development in BD: (i. Lack of education, ii. Deposit insecurity, iii. Lack of law)
Explanation: Bangladesh has a strong Cooperative Law (2001), so (iii) is false. Lack of education and insecurity are the real internal issues.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Benefits a patent holder receives: (i. Ownership, ii. Tax holiday, iii. Financial connection)
Explanation: Patents grant Exclusive Ownership (i) and help in Financial Linkages (iii) for commercialization; Tax holidays are usually investment incentives, not patent benefits.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law is suitable for protecting the rights of a book author?
Explanation: Copyright protects creative, literary, and artistic works like books and music.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Software Engineer Rahim sells his registered software for 50 lac. Another firm uses it illegally. Q: Which law was it registered under?
Explanation: Under the Bangladesh Copyright Act, Computer Software is specifically protected as a literary work.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: (Part 3) What will be the outcome of the lawsuit? (i. Compensation, ii. Injunction, iii. Destruction order)
Explanation: In copyright cases, the court can grant Compensation, stop the usage (Injunction), and order the destruction of illegal copies.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What is the primary function of BSTI?
Explanation: BSTI (BD Standards & Testing Institution) sets quality standards and ensures products in the market follow them.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law protects a book author's rights?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Creative works are covered by Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Harmful effects of soil pollution: (i. Heart attack, ii. Dysentery, iii. Skin disease)
Explanation: Soil pollution affects food/water, leading to Dysentery and Skin diseases; Heart attacks are more commonly linked to noise or air pollution.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which type of pollution is responsible for Typhoid?
Explanation: Typhoid is a water-borne disease caused by contaminated water consumption.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What is the 4th clause of the Memorandum of Association?
Explanation: Order of clauses: 1. Name, 2. Office, 3. Objective (sometimes 3rd or 4th depending on textbook, but standard is 3rd: Objective, 4th: Liability). Note: Standard check needed for specific text.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: How long is a book author's copyright protected?
Explanation: In Bangladesh, copyright for a literary work lasts for the Author's life + 60 years.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year was the BD Environment Conservation Act passed?
Explanation: The Environment Conservation Act was enacted in 1995.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: BSTI is NOT a member of which organization?
Explanation: BSTI is involved in standards (ISO, IEC, OIML). ILO (International Labour Org) deals with worker rights, not product standards.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which year is the current Copyright Act of Bangladesh?
Explanation: The governing law is the Copyright Act, 2000.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: If an image of a capital letter 'R' inside a triangle, it relates to?
Explanation: Distinctive signs, logos, or brand names are protected via Trademarks.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Harmful effects of Noise pollution: (i. Heart attack, ii. Stroke, iii. Skin disease)
Explanation: Noise pollution causes stress that leads to Heart attacks and Strokes; it does not cause skin disease.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law applies to the protection of artworks?
Explanation: Artistic works (paintings, sculptures, photos) are protected by Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year was the current Trademark Act passed in BD?
Explanation: The Trademarks Act, 2009 is the active legislation.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Where was the first Patent Law implemented?
Explanation: The first formal patent system (Venetian Statute) was established in Venice, Italy (1474).

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Who benefits from a Patent? (i. Inventor, ii. Importer, iii. ISO)
Explanation: Only the Inventor/Innovator (উদ্ভাবক) benefits from the exclusive rights of a patent.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Software Engineer Rohin sells software; 2 other firms use it. Q: Law of registration?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Software falls under the Copyright Act.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Software Eng. Rohin's software is used by others. Q: Outcome of the case? (i. Compensation, ii. Injunction, iii. Destruction order)
Explanation: Under Copyright law, the court can grant damages, stop illegal use, and order the destruction of pirated copies.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: BSTI is under which ministry?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) BSTI falls under the Ministry of Industries.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: ISO quality control fields: (i. Home banking, ii. Raw material ratio, iii. Supplier relations)
Explanation: ISO 9000 covers everything from manufacturing ratios to service standards and supply chain management.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Two chapters of Mr. Arup's book were copied in a guide. Q: What protection did he use?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Literary work (books) is protected by Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: (Part above) Reason for seeking civil court help: (i. Legal right, ii. Punishment, iii. Ban the book)
Explanation: The author has the legal right to punish the infringer and get the unauthorized book banned from the market.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Patent is what kind of asset?
Explanation: Patents, Copyrights, and Trademarks are Intellectual Property (Intangible assets).

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law is most effective for author protection?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Copyright is for authors.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Insurance generally removes which business barrier?
Explanation: Insurance handles the risk-related obstacles (ঝুঁকিগত প্রতিবন্ধকতা) in business.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year did the government enact the Copyright Act?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Copyright Act, 2000.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Kabir markets his spices under the name 'Sugandha'. Q: Which law protects him?
Explanation: A brand name used to identify a product is protected by Trademark.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Life Insurance is what type of contract? (i. Financial aid, ii. Guarantee, iii. Indemnity)
Explanation: Life insurance is a Contract of Guarantee/Assurance, whereas general insurance is a Contract of Indemnity (ক্ষতিপূরণের চুক্তি).

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Patent is which type of asset?
Explanation: Legally/Accountably, Intellectual Property is treated as an Intangible Fixed Asset.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: BSTI is under which ministry?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Industry Ministry.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Where is the head office of ISO located?
Explanation: The International Organization for Standardization (ISO) is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law protects the rights of a book author?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: Mr. Rudra's registered photos are used by a company for a calendar. Q: Legal remedy?
Explanation: Photography is an artistic work protected by Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Stem: (Part of above) Criminal financial penalty for the company?
Explanation: Under the Copyright Act 2000, the maximum fine for certain infringements is 2,00,000 BDT.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What helps distinguish a product from similar products?
Explanation: Trademark provides a unique identity to a brand.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which of the following is a benefit of a Patent?
Explanation: A Patent provides the inventor exclusive legal rights to their invention for a specific period.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What can buyers understand about a product through its Trademark?
Explanation: A Trademark helps consumers identify the source and consistent Quality of a product.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which of the following can be considered a Trademark?
Explanation: Words, symbols, logos, and labels used to distinguish goods are all Trademarks.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What is the initial duration of a Trademark registration?
Explanation: In Bangladesh, the initial registration is valid for 7 years, renewable thereafter.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year was the current Trademark Act enacted?
Explanation: The governing legislation is the Trademarks Act, 2009.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Trademark is what kind of business asset?
Explanation: Like patents, Trademarks are Intangible Assets (অদৃশ্যমান সম্পদ).

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: The 'TITAN' brand in the watch market refers to which of the following?
Explanation: TITAN is a brand name protected by Trademark laws.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which law protects artists or book authors?
Explanation: Creative works like books, music, and art fall under the Copyright Act.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What is the copyright term for works by govt or international bodies?
Explanation: In Bangladesh, the term is 60 years from the date of publication.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: What is the fine for intentionally violating Copyright law for the first time?
Explanation: The fine ranges from 50,000 to 2,00,000 BDT for a first-time intentional offense.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: If a songwriter applies for copyright but composer/singer differ—
Explanation: All contributors involved (composer/singer) must be notified of the claim.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which is NOT consistent with Copyright law?
Explanation: New Inventions are protected by Patents, not Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: For the first violation of Copyright, what is the minimum jail term?
Explanation: The criminal penalty for a first-time violation starts at 6 months of imprisonment.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year was the Noise Pollution Control Rules enacted in BD?
Explanation: The Noise Pollution (Control) Rules were established in 2006.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which of the following causes water pollution?
Explanation: Oil tanker leaks (তৈল ট্যাংকারে ফাটল) directly cause massive water pollution.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year did the ISO start its journey?
Explanation: The ISO was founded on February 23, 1947.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: When did ISO begin issuing the ISO 9000 certification?
Explanation: The ISO 9000 series for quality management was first published in 1987.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which of the following is NOT an ISO certification?
Explanation: ISO 9000, 9001, 9002, and 9003 are all part of the quality management family.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year did BSTI start its journey?
Explanation: BSTI was formed in its current state in 1985 via a presidential ordinance.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: A company sells watches under the brand 'Yamuna'. This name is a—
Explanation: A brand name used in commerce is a Trademark.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Poet Nirmalendu Goon wrote a book. Which law protects his rights?
Explanation: (Repeat Concept) Literary works are protected by Copyright.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Samsung released a handset; people buy it based on the name. This is—
Explanation: Buying based on brand trust/name recognition is the power of a Trademark.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: In which year was the Patent & Design Act enacted in BD?
Explanation: The core law for patents in Bangladesh is the Patents and Designs Act, 1911.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
Question: Which statement about Patents is NOT true?
Explanation: An inventor can grant licenses to others to use the patented invention for a fee/royalty.

Subject: Business Org & Mgmt 1st
